name: Task (Modern Makefile Alternative via Taskfile)

performance.notes: Go binary startup; negligible overhead vs task execution time

agent_readiness.notes: YAML-based task runner replacing Makefiles. Taskfile.yml: tasks: build: cmd: 'go build ./...'. Task dependencies, variables, dotenv, OS-specific cmds, parallelism (task --parallel). Fingerprinting (sources/generates) for incremental builds. Internal tasks. Includes (split Taskfiles). Shell completion. Cross-platform (no Make required on Windows). Used across languages (not just Go). Cleaner syntax than Makefile — no tab requirements, clearer variables. Popular in Go, Python, Node.js projects.

gotchas: Not as universal as Makefile (Makefile pre-installed on most systems)
Large Taskfiles can become complex — use includes
best_when: Projects wanting clean Makefile alternative with YAML syntax
Teams who find Makefile syntax frustrating
avoid_when: Projects with existing complex Makefiles (migration cost)
Environments where only Makefile is available
Simple projects where a few shell scripts suffice
summary: Task is a YAML-based Makefile alternative. Taskfile.yml defines tasks with deps, vars, sources for incremental builds, OS-specific commands, parallelism. Cross-platform (works on Windows without Make). Popular in Go, Python, Node projects. Cleaner than Makefile syntax. task build, task test, task deploy. Hierarchical includes for monorepos.
